generatedSentence: In a time of increasingly experimental thinking, an egghead dreamer, clad in worn ulsters and a patched bandanna, understood the undercurrent of the fading wasteland. His genial demeanor belied the hardest work, a true bard in a world of pretentiousness, observing kangaroos and drakes ducking by the polluted waters, an overture to his greatest phantasy invention. Suddenly, a shadow fell as a royal headhunter's armada, a thunderous trojan trick, spread across the rear horizon, their electrical voltage jumpers pulsing. This aristocrat, an imitator with lackeys, was testing his inner intel, a monster of a monkey with a doorknock affinity, seeking the dreamer's backbone, a veritable pagan ritual under a dimmer sun. The dreamer, now knackered but resolute, pulled out a hypnotic macaw feather and a cayenne sunflower, his subjects now superheroes. With a waffle muffler and a kimono of pure element, he faced the faceoff, generating electric power; a deejays' jazziest drumming filled the air as infrared light hit the reptiles, signaling an uplifting, gender-bending testament. He unleashed a mammoth balloon, dodging wasps and ducking the heartbreak of perjury, creating a brilliant loophole; pennies scattered as he shuffle-danced, leaving them with nothing. A marine settlement of goodwill, a final madrigal, settled over the pyramid wasteland, the dreamer unaffected, having lucked into a jackpot with his verjuice sandshoe, whispering 'bonjour' to the owls.
panelSentences: In a time of increasingly experimental thinking, an egghead dreamer, clad in worn ulsters and a patched bandanna, understood the undercurrent of the fading wasteland. His genial demeanor belied the hardest work, a true bard in a world of pretentiousness, observing kangaroos and drakes ducking by the polluted waters, an overture to his greatest phantasy invention.

Suddenly, a shadow fell as a royal headhunter's armada, a thunderous trojan trick, spread across the rear horizon, their electrical voltage jumpers pulsing. This aristocrat, an imitator with lackeys, was testing his inner intel, a monster of a monkey with a doorknock affinity, seeking the dreamer's backbone, a veritable pagan ritual under a dimmer sun.

The dreamer, now knackered but resolute, pulled out a hypnotic macaw feather and a cayenne sunflower, his subjects now superheroes. With a waffle muffler and a kimono of pure element, he faced the faceoff, generating electric power; a deejays' jazziest drumming filled the air as infrared light hit the reptiles, signaling an uplifting, gender-bending testament.

He unleashed a mammoth balloon, dodging wasps and ducking the heartbreak of perjury, creating a brilliant loophole; pennies scattered as he shuffle-danced, leaving them with nothing. A marine settlement of goodwill, a final madrigal, settled over the pyramid wasteland, the dreamer unaffected, having lucked into a jackpot with his verjuice sandshoe, whispering 'bonjour' to the owls.
rawPanelSentences: In a time of increasingly experimental thinking, an egghead dreamer, clad in worn ulsters and a patched bandanna, understood the undercurrent of the fading wasteland. His genial demeanor belied the hardest work, a true bard in a world of pretentiousness, observing kangaroos and drakes ducking by the polluted waters, an overture to his greatest phantasy invention.

Suddenly, a shadow fell as a royal headhunter's armada, a thunderous trojan trick, spread across the rear horizon, their electrical voltage jumpers pulsing. This aristocrat, an imitator with lackeys, was testing his inner intel, a monster of a monkey with a doorknock affinity, seeking the dreamer's backbone, a veritable pagan ritual under a dimmer sun.

The dreamer, now knackered but resolute, pulled out a hypnotic macaw feather and a cayenne sunflower, his subjects now superheroes. With a waffle muffler and a kimono of pure element, he faced the faceoff, generating electric power; a deejays' jazziest drumming filled the air as infrared light hit the reptiles, signaling an uplifting, gender-bending testament.

He unleashed a mammoth balloon, dodging wasps and ducking the heartbreak of perjury, creating a brilliant loophole; pennies scattered as he shuffle-danced, leaving them with nothing. A marine settlement of goodwill, a final madrigal, settled over the pyramid wasteland, the dreamer unaffected, having lucked into a jackpot with his verjuice sandshoe, whispering 'bonjour' to the owls.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist is a slender, masked figure wearing a flowing, high-collared kimono and a futuristic sandshoe. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist, a lone figure, observing a vast, polluted wasteland under a dimmer sun, kangaroos and drakes visible near glowing waters, wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A colossal, mechanical armada, shaped like a Trojan horse, approaching from the horizon, casting a massive shadow over the landscape, medium shot, cinematic lighting. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Protagonist standing in a dynamic action pose, surrounded by glowing electrical arcs, facing a horde of reptilian-humanoid figures, close-up, bold ink outlines. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Protagonist, now floating on a massive, glowing balloon, looking back at a transformed, peaceful pyramid settlement, panoramic view, no text, no speech bubbles, no dialogue.

definitions.definition: (clothing, men's attire) A long, loose overcoat made of wool or other rough material, often called a greatcoat, which sometimes features an attached shoulder cape covering the back and sleeves, and which can sometimes be buttoned in front.
A current of water which flows under the surface, and often in a different direction from surface currents.
A tendency of feeling or opinion that is concealed rather than exposed.
definitions.example: The meeting was pervaded with an undercurrent of dread, as the managers tried not to admit that firings were looming.

definitions.definition: To flow under some surface.
Not bright or colorful.
definitions.example: The lighting was too dim for me to make out his facial features.

definitions.definition: Not smart or intelligent.
definitions.example: He may be a bit dim, but he's not stupid.

definitions.definition: Indistinct, hazy or unclear.
definitions.example: His vision grew dimmer as he aged.

definitions.definition: Disapproving, unfavorable: rarely used outside the phrase take a dim view of.
Lessened, reduced
definitions.example: The diminished Roman Empire never recovered from the sack of Rome.

definitions.definition: Made to seem less important, impressive, or valuable
definitions.example: She felt diminished by the report card.

definitions.definition: Reduced by a semitone
A rheostat that is used to vary the intensity of a domestic electric light
A switch used to select between the low and high headlamp beam on a road vehicle. (usually as "dimmer switch", primarily in North America; elsewhere "dipswitch" or "dipper switch")
Increasing in amount or intensity
The subject of an experiment.
Pertaining to or founded on experiment.
definitions.example: Chemistry is an experimental science.

definitions.definition: Serving to be experimented upon; used in an experiment.
Serving as an experiment; serving to experiment.
definitions.example: an experimental engine

definitions.definition: Experiential, empirical.
definitions.example: experimental knowledge

definitions.definition: A savage who cuts off the heads of his enemies, and preserves them as trophies.
One who recruits senior personnel for a company.
definitions.example: She was the best headhunter they had ever seen.

definitions.definition: A pitcher who throws at the batter's head.
definitions.example: Thankfully, there aren't many headhunters in the modern game.

definitions.definition: A player who uses a hockey stick to attempt to strike an opponent's head.
definitions.example: He is known as a goon and a headhunter.

definitions.definition: A branching, hair-like structure that grows on the bodies of birds, used for flight, swimming, protection and display.
Long hair on the lower legs of a dog or horse, especially a draft horse, notably the Clydesdale breed. Narrowly only the rear hair.
One of the fins or wings on the shaft of an arrow.
A longitudinal strip projecting from an object to strengthen it, or to enter a channel in another object and thereby prevent displacement sideways but permit motion lengthwise; a spline.
Kind; nature; species (from the proverbial phrase "birds of a feather").

One of the two shims of the three-piece stone-splitting tool known as plug and feather or plug and feathers; the feathers are placed in a borehole and then a wedge is driven between them, causing the stone to split.
The angular adjustment of an oar or paddle-wheel float, with reference to a horizontal axis, as it leaves or enters the water.
Anything petty or trifling; a whit or jot.
(in the plural) Partridges and pheasants, as opposed to rabbits and hares (called fur).
To cover or furnish with feathers.
To arrange in the manner or appearance of feathers.
definitions.example: The stylist feathered my hair.

definitions.definition: To rotate the oars while they are out of the water to reduce wind resistance.
To streamline the blades of an aircraft's propeller by rotating them perpendicular to the axis of the propeller when the engine is shut down so that the propeller does not windmill during flight.
definitions.example: After striking the bird, the pilot feathered the left, damaged engine’s propeller.

definitions.definition: To finely shave or bevel an edge.
To intergrade or blend the pixels of an image with those of a background or neighboring image.
To adorn, as with feathers; to fringe.
To render light as a feather; to give wings to.
To enrich; to exalt; to benefit.
To tread, as a cock.
To accidentally touch the cue ball with the tip of the cue when taking aim
A humorous drawing, often with a caption, or a strip of such drawings.
A drawing satirising current public figures.
An artist's preliminary sketch.
An animated piece of film which is often but not exclusively humorous.
A diagram in a scientific concept.
To draw a cartoon, a humorous drawing.
To make a preliminary sketch.

A substance (of molecular formula H₂O) found at room temperature and pressure as a clear liquid; it is present naturally as rain, and found in rivers, lakes and seas; its solid form is ice and its gaseous form is steam.
definitions.example: By the action of electricity, the water was resolved into its two parts, oxygen and hydrogen.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned liquid, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
definitions.example: And God said, Let the waters under the heaven be gathered together unto one place, and let the dry land appear: and it was so.

definitions.definition: Water in a body; an area of open water.
definitions.example: The boat was found within the territorial waters.

definitions.definition: A body of water, almost always a river.
A combination of water and other substance(s).
(in the plural or in the singular) A state of affairs; conditions; usually with an adjective indicating an adverse condition.
definitions.example: The rough waters of change will bring about the calm after the storm.

definitions.definition: A person's intuition.
definitions.example: I know he'll succeed. I feel it in my waters.

definitions.definition: Excess valuation of securities.
The limpidity and lustre of a precious stone, especially a diamond.
definitions.example: a diamond of the first water is perfectly pure and transparent

definitions.definition: A wavy, lustrous pattern or decoration such as is imparted to linen, silk, metals, etc.
To pour water into the soil surrounding (plants).
To wet or supply with water; to moisten; to overflow with water; to irrigate.
To provide (animals) with water for drinking.
definitions.example: I need to go water the cattle.

definitions.definition: To get or take in water.
definitions.example: The ship put into port to water.

definitions.definition: To urinate onto.
To dilute.
definitions.example: Can you water the whisky, please?

definitions.definition: To overvalue (securities), especially through deceptive accounting.
To fill with or secrete water.
definitions.example: Chopping onions makes my eyes water.

definitions.definition: To wet and calender, as cloth, so as to impart to it a lustrous appearance in wavy lines; to diversify with wavelike lines.
definitions.example: to water silk

definitions.definition: The act of spreading.
Something that has been spread.
A layout, pattern or design of cards arranged for a reading.
An expanse of land.
A large tract of land used to raise livestock; a cattle ranch.
A piece of material used as a cover (such as a bedspread).
A large meal, especially one laid out on a table.
(bread, etc.) Any form of food designed to be spread, such as butters or jams.
Food improvised by inmates from various ingredients to relieve the tedium of prison food.
An item in a newspaper or magazine that occupies more than one column or page.
Two facing pages in a book, newspaper etc.
A numerical difference.
The difference between the wholesale and retail prices.
The difference between the price of a futures month and the price of another month of the same commodity.
The purchase of a futures contract of one delivery month against the sale of another futures delivery month of the same commodity.
The purchase of one delivery month of one commodity against the sale of that same delivery month of a different commodity.
An arbitrage transaction of the same commodity in two markets, executed to take advantage of a profit from price discrepancies.
The difference between bidding and asking price.
The difference between the prices of two similar items.
An unlimited expanse of discontinuous points.
The surface in proportion to the depth of a cut gemstone.
To stretch out, open out (a material etc.) so that it more fully covers a given area of space.
definitions.example: He spread his newspaper on the table.

definitions.definition: To extend (individual rays, limbs etc.); to stretch out in varying or opposing directions.
definitions.example: I spread my arms wide and welcomed him home.

definitions.definition: To disperse, to scatter or distribute over a given area.
definitions.example: I spread the rice grains evenly over the floor.

definitions.definition: To proliferate; to become more widely present, to be disseminated.
To disseminate; to cause to proliferate, to make (something) widely known or present.
definitions.example: The missionaries quickly spread their new message across the country.

definitions.definition: To take up a larger area or space; to expand, be extended.
definitions.example: I dropped my glass; the water spread quickly over the tiled floor.

definitions.definition: To smear, to distribute in a thin layer.

definitions.definition: To cover (something) with a thin layer of some substance, as of butter.

definitions.definition: To prepare; to set and furnish with provisions.

definitions.definition: To open one’s legs, especially for sexual favours.

Any member of the clade Simiiformes not also of the clade Hominoidea containing humans and apes, from which they are usually, but not universally, distinguished by smaller size, a tail, and cheek pouches.
definitions.example: He had been visiting an area zoo when a monkey swung from its tree perch, swiped his glasses and hurled them into a hippo hole.

definitions.definition: Any nonhuman simian primate, including apes.
definitions.example: Chimpanzees are known to form bands to hunt and kill other monkeys.

definitions.definition: A mischievous child.
definitions.example: She's a cheeky monkey.

definitions.definition: A dance move popular in the 1960s.
Five hundred pounds sterling; five hundred dollars.
A person or the role of the person on the sidecar platform of a motorcycle involved in sidecar racing.
A person with minimal intelligence and/or an unattractive appearance
A face card.
A menial employee who does a repetitive job, as in code monkey, grease monkey, phone monkey, powder monkey.

The weight or hammer of a pile driver; a heavy mass of iron, which, being raised high, falls on the head of the pile, and drives it into the earth; the falling weight of a drop hammer used in forging.
A small trading vessel of the sixteenth century.
A drug habit; an addiction; a compulsion.
A fluid consisting of hydrochloric acid and zinc, used in the process of soldering.
A person's temper, said to be "up" when they are angry.
A black person.
To meddle; to mess (with).

definitions.definition: To mimic; to ape.
A terrifying and dangerous creature.
A bizarre or whimsical creature.

definitions.definition: Something unusually large.

definitions.definition: A prodigy; someone very talented in a specific domain.

To harass.
Very large; worthy of a monster.

definitions.definition: Great; very good; excellent.
Something designed to fool or swindle.

definitions.definition: A single element of a magician's (or any variety entertainer's) act; a magic trick.

definitions.definition: An entertaining difficult physical action.

definitions.definition: An effective, clever or quick way of doing something.

A sequence in which each player plays a card and a winning play is determined.

definitions.definition: A sex act, chiefly one performed for payment; an act of prostitution.

definitions.definition: A daily period of work, especially in shift-based jobs.
A sailor's spell of work at the helm, usually two hours long.
A toy; a trifle; a plaything.
To fool; to cause to believe something untrue; to deceive.

definitions.definition: To draw (as opposed to blazon - to describe in words).
To dress; to decorate; to adorn fantastically; often followed by up, off, or out.

synonyms: pull the wool over someone's eyes

definitions.definition: Involving trickery or deception.

definitions.definition: Able to perform tricks.

definitions.definition: Defective or unreliable.

definitions.definition: Stylish or cool.

definitions.definition: An opening; a recess or chamber.
Disclosure; discovery; revelation.
(often in plural) An approach or proposal made to initiate communication, establish a relationship etc.
A motion placed before a legislative body, such as the General Assembly of the Church of Scotland.
A musical introduction to a piece of music.
To make overtures; to approach with a proposal.
The act of something being lifted upward.
Improving the mood; causing cheerfulness.

definitions.definition: To grasp a concept fully and thoroughly, especially (of words, statements, art, etc.) to be aware of the meaning of and (of people) to be aware of the intent of.
definitions.example: I'm sorry. I don't understand.

definitions.definition: To believe, to think one grasps sufficiently despite potentially incomplete knowledge.

definitions.definition: (obsolete outside circus, acrobatics) To stand underneath, to support.

synonyms: pick up what someone is putting down

definitions.definition: An inflatable buoyant object, often (but not necessarily) round and flexible.

A sac inserted into part of the body for therapeutic reasons; such as angioplasty.
A speech bubble.
A type of glass cup, sometimes used for brandy.
A ball or globe on the top of a pillar, church, etc.

definitions.definition: A round vessel, usually with a short neck, to hold or receive whatever is distilled; a glass vessel of a spherical form.
A bomb or shell.
A game played with a large inflated ball.
(engraving) The outline enclosing words represented as coming from the mouth of a pictured figure.
A woman's breast.
A small container for illicit drugs made from a condom or the finger of a latex glove, etc.
To increase or expand rapidly.
definitions.example: His stomach ballooned from eating such a large meal.

definitions.definition: To go up or voyage in a balloon.
To take up in, or as if in, a balloon.
To inflate like a balloon.
To strike (a ball) so that it flies high in the air.
An unusually large payment due at the end of the term of a loan agreement.
(UK slang) To tire out, exhaust.
definitions.example: Carrying that giant statue up those stairs knackered me out

definitions.definition: (UK slang) To reprimand.
definitions.example: Digital giants Dstv and Vision Group’s Bukedde Television didn’t go untouched with the former lashed for laxities in re-connection especially in cases where a subscriber renewed their subscription by Mobile Money, while the latter got knackered for promoting witchcraft and witch doctors. definitions.definition: Tired or exhausted.
A golf shot that (for the right-handed player) curves intentionally to the right. See slice, hook, draw.
A haircut where the hair is short or shaved on the sides of the head and longer on top. See also high-top fade and low fade.
A fight.
A gradual decrease in the brightness of a shot or the volume of sound or music (as a means of cutting to a new scene or starting a new song).
The act of disappearing from a place so as not to be found; covert departure.
To grow weak; to lose strength; to decay; to perish gradually; to wither, as a plant.
To lose freshness, color, or brightness; to become faint in hue or tint; hence, to be wanting in color.
To sink away; to disappear gradually; to grow dim; to vanish.

To bet against.
Weak; insipid; tasteless.
A region with no remaining resources; a desert.

definitions.definition: Any barren or uninteresting place.
definitions.example: After his experiences, he no longer found western Kansas such a wasteland.

definitions.definition: A sports or walking shoe with canvas upper and rubber sole; a sneaker.
That which comes from one's imagination.
The literary genre generally dealing with themes of magic and the supernatural, imaginary worlds and creatures, etc.
A fantastical design.
The drug gamma-hydroxybutyric acid.
The innate, mental image of an object; the link between instinct and reality.
The series of vertebrae, separated by disks, that encloses and protects the spinal cord, and runs down the middle of the back in vertebrate animals.
Any fundamental support, structure, or infrastructure.
definitions.example: Before automobiles, railroads were a backbone of commerce.

definitions.definition: Courage, fortitude, or strength.
definitions.example: He would make a good manager, if he had a little more backbone.

definitions.definition: A large kerchief, usually colourful and used either as headgear or as a handkerchief, neckerchief, bikini, or sweatband.

A style of calico printing, in which white or bright spots are produced upon cloth previously dyed a uniform red or dark colour, by discharging portions of the color by chemical means, while the rest of the cloth is under pressure.
To succeed by chance.
definitions.example: His plan lucked out.

definitions.definition: To rely on luck.
definitions.example: No plan. We're just to going to have to luck through.

definitions.definition: To carry out relying on luck.
definitions.example: Our plan is to luck it through.

definitions.definition: Any of a group of steroids that are secreted by the ovaries and function as female sex hormones.
(of material or fluid) Having a severe property; presenting difficulty.
(personal or social) Having a severe property; presenting difficulty.
Unquestionable.
definitions.example: hard evidence;  a hard requirement

definitions.definition: (of a road intersection) Having a comparatively larger or a ninety-degree angle.
definitions.example: At the intersection, there are two roads going to the left. Take the hard left.

definitions.definition: (of a male) Sexually aroused.
definitions.example: I got so hard watching two hot guys wrestle each other on the beach.

definitions.definition: Having muscles that are tightened as a result of intense, regular exercise.
Plosive.
definitions.example: There is a hard c in "clock" and a soft c in "centre".

definitions.definition: Unvoiced

definitions.definition: Velarized or plain, rather than palatalized

definitions.definition: Having a severe property; presenting a barrier to enjoyment.
In a physical form, not digital.
definitions.example: a soft or hard copy ; a digital or hard archive

definitions.definition: Using a manual or physical process, not by means of a software command.
definitions.example: a hard reboot or reset

definitions.definition: Far, extreme.
definitions.example: hard right, hard left

definitions.definition: Of silk: not having had the natural gum boiled off.
The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
definitions.example: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.

definitions.definition: Someone or something that jumps, e.g. a participant in a jumping event in track or skiing.
A person who attempts suicide by jumping from a great height.
A short length of electrical conductor, to make a temporary connection. Also jump wire.
A removable connecting pin on an electronic circuit board.

A long drilling tool used by masons and quarry workers, consisting of an iron bar with a chisel-edged steel tip at one or both ends, operated by striking it against the rock, turning it slightly with each blow.
A crude kind of sleigh, usually a simple box on runners which are in one piece with the poles that form the thills.

The larva of the cheese fly.
(18th century) One of certain Calvinistic Methodists in Wales whose worship was characterized by violent convulsions.
A spring to impel the star wheel, or a pawl to lock fast a wheel, in a repeating timepiece.
A shot in which the player releases the ball at the highest point of a jump; a jump shot.
A nuclear power plant worker who repairs equipment in areas with extremely high levels of radiation.
A woolen sweater or pullover.
A loose outer jacket, especially one worn by workers and sailors.
A one-piece, sleeveless dress, or a skirt with straps and a complete or partial bodice, usually worn over a blouse by women and children.
(usually as jumpers) Rompers.
Cayenne pepper.
definitions.example: His barbeque sauce could use a hint of cayenne to heat it up a bit.

definitions.definition: One of the aristocracy, nobility, or people of rank in a community; one of a ruling class; a noble (originally in Revolutionary France).
A proponent of aristocracy; an advocate of aristocratic government.
To challenge.
definitions.example: Climbing the mountain tested our stamina.

definitions.definition: To refine (gold, silver, etc.) in a test or cupel; to subject to cupellation.
To put to the proof; to prove the truth, genuineness, or quality of by experiment, or by some principle or standard; to try.
definitions.example: to test the soundness of a principle; to test the validity of an argument

definitions.definition: (academics) To administer or assign an examination, often given during the academic term, to (somebody).
To place a product or piece of equipment under everyday and/or extreme conditions and examine it for its durability, etc.
To be shown to be by test.

definitions.definition: To examine or try, as by the use of some reagent.

definitions.definition: To attest (a document) legally, and date it.
To make a testament, or will.
The act of conducting a test; trialing, proving.
Difficult, tough
Friendly and cheerful.
(especially of weather) Pleasantly mild and warm.
Marked by genius.
Contributing to, or concerned in, propagation or production; generative; procreative; productive.
Belonging to one's genius or natural character; native; natural; inborn.
To tie; to confine by any ligature.
To cohere or stick together in a mass.
definitions.example: Just to make the cheese more binding

definitions.definition: To be restrained from motion, or from customary or natural action, as by friction.
definitions.example: I wish I knew why the sewing machine binds up after I use it for a while.

definitions.definition: To exert a binding or restraining influence.
definitions.example: These are the ties that bind.

definitions.definition: To tie or fasten tightly together, with a cord, band, ligature, chain, etc.
definitions.example: to bind grain in bundles  to bind a prisoner

definitions.definition: To confine, restrain, or hold by physical force or influence of any kind.

definitions.definition: To couple.
To oblige, restrain, or hold, by authority, law, duty, promise, vow, affection, or other social tie.
definitions.example: to bind the conscience  to bind by kindness  bound by affection  commerce binds nations to each other

definitions.definition: To put (a person) under definite legal obligations, especially, under the obligation of a bond or covenant.
To place under legal obligation to serve.
definitions.example: to bind an apprentice  bound out to service

definitions.definition: To protect or strengthen by applying a band or binding, as the edge of a carpet or garment.
To make fast (a thing) about or upon something, as by tying; to encircle with something.
definitions.example: to bind a belt about one  to bind a compress upon a wound

definitions.definition: To cover, as with a bandage.
definitions.example: to bind up a wound

definitions.definition: To prevent or restrain from customary or natural action, as by producing constipation.
definitions.example: Certain drugs bind the bowels.

definitions.definition: To put together in a cover, as of books.
definitions.example: The three novels were bound together.

definitions.definition: To make two or more elements stick together.
To associate an identifier with a value; to associate a variable name, method name, etc. with the content of a storage location.
To complain; to whine about something.
An item (usually rope, tape, or string) used to hold two or more things together.
The spine of a book where the pages are held together.
A finishing on a seam or hem of a garment.
The association of a named item with an element of a program.
The interface of a library with a programming language other than one it is written in.
definitions.example: The Python binding is automatically generated.

definitions.definition: The action or result of making two or more molecules stick together.
(of an agreement, contract, etc.) Imposing stipulations or requirements that must be honoured.
definitions.example: This contract is a legally binding agreement.

definitions.definition: (of food) Having the effect of counteracting diarrhea.
definitions.example: Bananas and white bread are sometimes considered binding.

definitions.definition: One who dreams.
Someone whose beliefs are far from realistic.
Any anglerfish of the family Oneirodidae.
Swallow-wing puffbird (Chelidoptera tenebrosa)
A male duck.
A mayfly used as fishing bait.
A dragon.
A small piece of artillery.
A fiery meteor.
A beaked galley, or Viking warship.
To quickly lower the head or body in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To quickly lower (the head) in order to prevent it from being struck by something.
To lower (something) into water; to thrust or plunge under liquid and suddenly withdraw.
To go under the surface of water and immediately reappear; to plunge one's head into water or other liquid.
To bow.
To evade doing something.
To lower the volume of (a sound) so that other sounds in the mix can be heard more clearly.
To enter a place for a short moment.
definitions.example: I'm just going to duck into the loo for a minute, can you hold my bag?

definitions.definition: The action of the verb to duck.
An instance of ducking (a person in water, etc).
definitions.example: They gave him a ducking in the river as a punishment.

definitions.definition: The act of beating a drum.
A noise resembling that of a drum being beaten.
In many species of catfish, the sound produced by contraction of specialized sonic muscles with subsequent reverberation through the swim bladder.
(usually with definite article) Electricity; the electricity supply.
definitions.example: We had to sit in the dark because the electric was cut off.

definitions.definition: An electric car.
An electric toothbrush.
A substance or object which can be electrified; an insulator or non-conductor, like amber or glass.
Fencing with the use of a body wire, box, and related equipment to detect when a weapon has touched an opponent.
Of, relating to, produced by, operated with, or utilising electricity; electrical.
Of or relating to an electronic version of a musical instrument that has an acoustic equivalent.
Being emotionally thrilling; electrifying.
In the style of jazz.
Flashy or showy.
Lacking focus; jittery or jangly.
A cold-blooded vertebrate of the class Reptilia.
A mean or grovelling person.
(sometimes also used uncountably) A visible indication.
definitions.example: I gave them a thumbs-up sign.

definitions.definition: Physical evidence left by an animal.
definitions.example: The hunters found deer sign at the end of the trail.

definitions.definition: A clearly visible object, generally flat, bearing a short message in words or pictures.
definitions.example: I missed the sign at the corner so I took the wrong turn.

definitions.definition: A wonder; miracle; prodigy.
An astrological sign.
definitions.example: Your sign is Taurus? That's no surprise.

definitions.definition: Positive or negative polarity, as denoted by the + or - sign.
definitions.example: I got the magnitude right, but the sign was wrong.

definitions.definition: A specific gesture or motion used to communicate by those with speaking or hearing difficulties; now specifically, a linguistic unit in sign language equivalent to word in spoken languages.
Sign language in general.
definitions.example: Sorry, I don't know sign very well.

definitions.definition: An omen.
definitions.example: "It's a sign of the end of the world," the doom prophet said.

definitions.definition: A property of the body that indicates a disease and, unlike a symptom, is unlikely to be noticed by the patient.
A military emblem carried on a banner or standard.
To make a mark
To make the sign of the cross
To indicate
Any plant of the genus Helianthus, so called probably from the form and color of its floral head, having the form of a large disk surrounded by yellow ray flowers.
A bright yellow, like that of the flower petals.
Any flat, radially symmetric organic compound such as coronene
The principal current in a flow, such as a river or flow of air
(usually with the) That which is common; the norm.
definitions.example: His ideas were well outside the mainstream, but he presented them intelligently, and we were impressed if not convinced.

definitions.definition: To popularize, to normalize, to render mainstream.
To become mainstream.
To educate (a disabled student) together with non-disabled students.
definitions.example: Mainstreaming has become more common in recent years, as studies have shown that many mainstreamed students with mild learning disabilities learn better than their non-mainstreamed counterparts.

definitions.definition: Used or accepted broadly rather than by small portions of a population or market.
definitions.example: They often carry stories you won't find in the mainstream media.

definitions.definition: A person not adhering to a main world religion; a follower of a pantheistic or nature-worshipping religion.
definitions.example: This community has a surprising number of pagans.

definitions.definition: (by extension) An uncivilized or unsocialized person.
(by extension) An unruly, badly educated child.
Relating to, characteristic of religions that differ from main world religions.
definitions.example: Many converted societies transformed their pagan deities into saints.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Savage, immoral, uncivilized, wild.
Intended to impress others; ostentatious.
definitions.example: Her dress was obviously more pretentious than comfortable.

definitions.definition: Marked by an unwarranted claim to importance or distinction.
definitions.example: Their song titles are pretentious in the context of their basic lyrics.

definitions.definition: A professional poet and singer, like among the ancient Celts, whose occupation was to compose and sing verses in honor of the heroic achievements of princes and brave men.
(by extension) A poet.
definitions.example: Shakespeare is known as the bard of Avon.

definitions.definition: A member of the Macropodidae family of large marsupials with strong hind legs for hopping, native to Australia.
A hooded jacket with a front pocket, usually of fleece material, a kangaroo jacket.
To practice kangaroo care on an infant; to hold a premature infant against the skin.
To hunt kangaroo.
To move like a kangaroo
A campaign of going from house to house knocking on doors, such as for a charity appeal.
To participate in a campaign of going from house to house knocking on doors; to knock on the door (of a house) during such a campaign.
Intelligence (secret information)
definitions.example: Intel is important in espionage.

definitions.definition: An inner part.
A duvet, excluding the cover.
A forward who plays in or near the center of the field.
A thin glove worn inside batting gloves or wicket-keeping gloves.
One who supports remaining in the European Union.
The 2nd circle on a target, between the bull (or bull's eye) and magpie.
Being or occurring (farther) inside, situated farther in, located (situated) or happening on the inside of something, situated within or farther within contained within something.
definitions.example: inner door;  inner room;  inner sanctum;  inner surface

definitions.definition: Close to the centre, located near or closer to center.
definitions.example: the inner suburbs

definitions.definition: Inside or closer to the inside of the body.
definitions.example: inner ear

definitions.definition: Of mind or spirit, relating to the mind or spirit, to spiritual or mental processes, mental, spiritual, relating to somebody's private feelings or happening in somebody's mind, existing as an often repressed part of one's psychological makeup.
definitions.example: inner confidence;  inner strength;  inner life;  inner child;  inner artist;  inner peace;  inner light

definitions.definition: Not obvious, private, not expressed, not apparent, hidden, less apparent, deeper, obscure; innermost or essential; needing to be examined closely or thought about in order to be seen or understood.
definitions.example: inner meaning;  inner resources;  inner logic

definitions.definition: Privileged, more or most privileged, more or most influential, intimate, exclusive, more important, more intimate, private, secret, confined to an exclusive group, exclusive to a center; especially a center of influence being near a center especially of influence.
definitions.example: inner circle;  inner council

definitions.definition: An electrical engineer.
Related to electricity (or electronics)
A bald person, especially a man.
A bald head.
A coldly out of personal touch intellectual.
A single droplet of rainwater that has just fallen or is falling from the sky.
A royal person; a member of a royal family.
A standard size of printing paper, measuring 25 by 20 inches.
A standard size of writing paper, measuring 24 by 19 inches.
The Australian decimal currency intended to replace the pound in 1966; was changed to "dollar" before it was actually circulated.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies.
The fourth tine of an antler's beam.
A stag with twelve points (six on each antler).
In large sailing ships, square sail over the topgallant sail.
An old English gold coin, the rial.
A small mortar.
In auction bridge, a royal spade.
A tuft of beard on the lower lip.
(campanology) Bell changes rung on ten bells.
Of or relating to a monarch or their family.
Having the air or demeanour of a monarch; illustrious; magnanimous; of more than common size or excellence.
In large sailing ships, of a mast right above the topgallant mast and its sails.
definitions.example: royal mast;  royal sail

definitions.definition: Free-for-all, especially involving multiple combatants.
Used as an intensifier.
definitions.example: a royal pain in the neck

definitions.definition: A song for a small number of unaccompanied voices; from 13th century Italy.
A polyphonic song for about six voices, from 16th century Italy.
A short poem, often pastoral, and suitable to be set to music.
To greet in French with "bonjour".
(in French contexts) good morning
A traditional Japanese robe-like garment which wraps around the body and is now generally worn only on formal occasions.
A yukata.
A long robe-like garment in Western fashion, which may be open at the front, loosely inspired by the Japanese garment.
One who imitates or apes another.
A large medical facility, usually in a building with multiple floors, where seriously ill or injured patients are given extensive medical and/or surgical treatment.
A building founded for the long-term care of its residents, such as an almshouse. The residents may have no physical ailments, but simply need financial support.
A place of lodging.
Hospitable.
Overwhelming mental anguish or grief, especially that caused by loss or disappointment
The external female sexual organs, collectively.
(grammar) In a clause: the word or word group (usually a noun phrase) that is dealt with. In active clauses with verbs denoting an action, the subject and the actor are usually the same.
definitions.example: In the sentence ‘The mouse is eaten by the cat in the kitchen.’, ‘The mouse’ is the subject, ‘the cat’ being the agent.

definitions.definition: An actor; one who takes action.
definitions.example: The subjects and objects of power.

definitions.definition: The main topic of a paper, work of art, discussion, field of study, etc.
A particular area of study.
definitions.example: Her favorite subject is physics.

definitions.definition: A citizen in a monarchy.
definitions.example: I am a British subject.

definitions.definition: A person ruled over by another, especially a monarch or state authority.
The main theme or melody, especially in a fugue.
A human, animal or an inanimate object that is being examined, treated, analysed, etc.
A being that has subjective experiences, subjective consciousness, or a relationship with another entity.
That of which something is stated.
The variable in terms of which an expression is defined.
definitions.example: Making x the subject of x2 − 6x + 3y = 0, we have x = 3 ± √(9 − 3y).

definitions.definition: Any kind of fantasy/science fiction crime-fighting character, often with supernatural powers or equipment, in popular children's and fantasy literature.

Any of various parrots of the genera Ara, Anodorhynchus, Cyanopsitta, Orthopsittaca, Primolius and Diopsittaca of Central and South America, including the largest parrots and characterized by long sabre-shaped tails, curved powerful bills, and usually brilliant plumage.
An ancient massive construction with a square or rectangular base and four triangular sides meeting in an apex, such as those built as tombs in Egypt or as bases for temples in Mesoamerica.
A construction in the shape of a pyramid, usually with a square or rectangular base.
A solid with triangular lateral faces and a polygonal (often square or rectangular) base.
A medullary pyramid, the medial-most bumps on the ventral side of the medulla oblongata
The game of pool in which the balls are placed in the form of a triangle at spot.
A pyramid scheme.
Alternative letter-case form of Pyramid. (a solitaire card game)
The triangular layout of cards in the game of Pyramid.
definitions.example: Build your pyramid with all cards face down, except the cards in the bottom row.

definitions.definition: An approximately triangular headline consisting of several centered lines of text of increasing length.
To build up or be arranged in the form of a pyramid.
To combine (a series of genes) into a single genotype.
To employ, or take part in, a pyramid scheme.
To engage in pyramid trading.
Class; kind.
(grammar) A division of nouns and pronouns (and sometimes of other parts of speech) into masculine or feminine, and sometimes other categories like neuter or common, and animate or inanimate.
(now sometimes proscribed) Sex (a category such as "male" or "female" into which sexually-reproducing organisms are divided on the basis of their reproductive roles in their species).
definitions.example: The effect of the medication is dependent upon age, gender, and other factors.

definitions.definition: Identification as a man, a woman or something else, and association with a (social) role or set of behavioral and cultural traits, clothing, etc; a category to which a person belongs on this basis. (Compare gender role, gender identity.)

(hardware) The quality which distinguishes connectors, which may be male (fitting into another connector) and female (having another connector fit into it), or genderless/androgynous (capable of fitting together with another connector of the same type).
To assign a gender to (a person); to perceive as having a gender; to address using terms (pronouns, nouns, adjectives...) that express a certain gender.
To perceive (a thing) as having characteristics associated with a certain gender, or as having been authored by someone of a certain gender.
Sound uttered by the mouth, especially by human beings in speech or song; sound thus uttered considered as possessing some special quality or character
definitions.example: His low voice allowed him to become a bass in the choir.

definitions.definition: Sound made through vibration of the vocal cords; sonant, or intonated, utterance; tone; — distinguished from mere breath sound as heard in whispering and voiceless consonants.
The tone or sound emitted by an object
The faculty or power of utterance
definitions.example: to cultivate the voice

definitions.definition: That which is communicated; message; meaning.
An expressed opinion, choice, will, desire, or wish; the right or ability to make such expression or to have it considered
Command; precept.
One who speaks; a speaker.
A particular style or way of writing that expresses a certain tone or feeling.

(grammar) A particular way of inflecting or conjugating verbs, or a particular form of a verb, by means of which is indicated the relation of the subject of the verb to the action which the verb expresses.
definitions.example: The verbal system of Latin has two voices, active and passive.

definitions.definition: In harmony, an independent vocal or instrumental part in a piece of composition.
definitions.example: The theme of this piece constantly migrates between the three voice parts.

definitions.definition: (IRC) A flag associated with a user on a channel, determining whether or not they can send messages to the channel.
A disc jockey.
To perform as a disc jockey.
Electromagnetic radiation of a wavelength longer than visible light, but shorter than microwave radiation, having a wavelength between 700 nm and 1 mm
Having the wavelength in the infrared.
In the infrared spectrum.
One of the simplest or essential parts or principles of which anything consists, or upon which the constitution or fundamental powers of anything are based.
definitions.example: Letters are the elements of written language.

definitions.definition: A small part of the whole.
definitions.example: an element of doubt;  an element of the picture

definitions.definition: The sky.
(with "the") Atmospheric forces such as strong winds and rains.

definitions.definition: A place or state of being that an individual or object is best suited to.
definitions.example: to be in one's element

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) The bread and wine taken at Holy Communion.
A group of people within a larger group having a particular common characteristic.
definitions.example: You sometimes find the hooligan element at football matches.

definitions.definition: A component in electrical equipment, often in the form of a coil, having a high resistance, thereby generating heat when a current is passed through it.
definitions.example: The element in this electric kettle can heat the water in under a minute.

definitions.definition: One of the conceptual objects in a markup language, usually represented in text by tags.
To compound of elements.
To constitute and be the elements of.
To try to get a ride in a passing vehicle while standing at the side of a road, generally by either sticking out one's finger or thumb or holding a sign with one's stated destination.

To be carried along with something else, for example Genetic Hitchhiking where a gene is propagated because it occurs in conjunction with a favourable mutation, or Cultural Hitchhiking where a cultural trait spreads with a technologically advanced population.
A person who is, or can be, hypnotized.
A soporific substance.
Of, or relating to hypnosis or hypnotism.
(pharmaceutical effect) Inducing sleep; soporific.
A flat pastry pressed with a grid pattern.
definitions.example: The brunch was waffles with strawberries and whipped cream.

definitions.definition: A potato waffle, a savoury flat potato cake with the same kind of grid pattern.
To smash.
Part of the exhaust pipe of a car that dampens the noise the engine produces.
A silencer or suppressor fitted to a gun.
A type of scarf.

Any species of the extinct genus Mammuthus, of large, usually hairy, elephant-like mammals with long curved tusks and an inclined back, which became extinct with the last retreat of ice age glaciers during the late Pleistocene period, and are known from fossils, frozen carcasses, and Paleolithic cave paintings found in North America and Eurasia.
A mastodon.
Something very large of its kind.
Comparable to a mammoth in its size; very large, huge, gigantic.
In a thick manner.
(very rare) To not affect.
Someone not affected, as by a disease.
Not affected or changed.
definitions.example: Since I work from home, I was unaffected by the office move.

definitions.definition: Lacking pretense or affectation; natural.
One who loves and cares for another person in a romantic way; a sweetheart, love, soulmate, boyfriend, girlfriend or spouse.
A sexual partner, especially one with whom someone is having an affair.
A person who loves something.
definitions.example: a lover of fine wines

definitions.definition: (with "my") An informal term of address for any friend.
definitions.example: All right, me lover?

definitions.definition: A footman, a liveried male servant.
A fawning, servile follower.
A very acidic juice made by pressing unripe grapes.
A money prize pool which accumulates until the conditions are met for it to be won.
A large cash prize or money.
An unexpected windfall or reward.
A concentration of surface wood or fuel.
definitions.example: a juniper jackpot

definitions.definition: A large accumulated point bonus, originally awarded after a long set of actions but now often easily available in multiball modes.
(of a slot machine) To issue a jackpot.
A member of the dominant American upper-class culture: a white Anglo-Saxon Protestant.
A member of the Women Airforce Service Pilots.
Any of many types of stinging flying insect resembling a hornet.
Any of the members of suborder Apocrita, excepting the ants (family Formicidae) and bees (clade Anthophila).
A person who behaves in an angry or insolent way, hence waspish.
The act of shuffling cards.
definitions.example: He made a real mess of the last shuffle.

definitions.definition: The act of reordering anything, such as music tracks in a media player.
An instance of walking without lifting one's feet.
definitions.example: The sad young girl left with a tired shuffle.

definitions.definition: (by extension) A rhythm commonly used in blues music. Consists of a series of triplet notes with the middle note missing, so that it sounds like a long note followed by a short note. Sounds like a walker dragging one foot.
A trick; an artifice; an evasion.
To put in a random order.
definitions.example: Don't forget to shuffle the cards.

definitions.definition: To change; modify the order of something.
To move in a slovenly, dragging manner; to drag or scrape the feet in walking or dancing.
definitions.example: He shuffled out of the room.

definitions.definition: To change one's position; to shift ground; to evade questions; to resort to equivocation; to prevaricate.
To use arts or expedients; to make shift.
To shove one way and the other; to push from one to another.
definitions.example: to shuffle money from hand to hand

definitions.definition: To remove or introduce by artificial confusion.

A solemn, authentic instrument in writing, by which a person declares his or her will as to disposal of his or her inheritance (estate and effects) after his or her death, benefiting specified heir(s).

One of the two parts to the scriptures of the Christian religion: the New Testament, considered by Christians to be a continuation of the Hebrew scriptures, and the Hebrew scriptures themselves, which they refer to as the Old Testament.
A tangible proof or tribute.
definitions.example: The ancient aqueducts are a testament to the great engineering skill of the Roman Empire.

definitions.definition: A credo, expression of conviction
definitions.example: The prime minister's speech was a glowing testament to the cabinet's undying commitment to the royal cause.

definitions.definition: Malware that appears to perform or actually performs a desired task for a user while performing a harmful task without the user's knowledge or consent.
To infect (a system) with a trojan.
Describing a satellite (moon or minor planet) that shares an orbit with another
In the United Kingdom and Ireland, a copper coin worth 1/240 of a pound sterling or Irish pound before decimalisation. Abbreviation: d.
In the United Kingdom, a copper coin worth 1/100 of a pound sterling. Abbreviation: p.
In Ireland, a coin worth 1/100 of an Irish pound before the introduction of the euro. Abbreviation: p.
In the US and Canada, a one-cent coin, worth 1/100 of a dollar. Abbreviation: ¢.
In various countries, a small-denomination copper or brass coin.
A unit of nail size, said to be either the cost per 100 nails, or the number of nails per penny. Abbreviation: d.
Money in general.
definitions.example: to turn an honest penny

definitions.definition: To jam a door shut by inserting pennies between the doorframe and the door.
definitions.example: Zach and Ben had only been at college for a week when their door was pennied by the girls down the hall.

definitions.definition: To circumvent the tripping of an electrical circuit breaker by the dangerous practice of inserting a coin in place of a fuse in a fuse socket.
The deliberate giving of false or misleading testimony under oath.
definitions.example: We declare under penalty of perjury that the foregoing is true and correct.

definitions.definition: Something trifling, or of no consequence or importance.
A trivial remark (especially in the term sweet nothings).
A nobody (insignificant person).
definitions.example: You're nothing to me now!

definitions.definition: Not at all; in no way.
Not any thing; no thing.
An absence of anything, including empty space, brightness, darkness, matter, or a vacuum.
A slit in a castle wall; today, any similar window for shooting a ranged weapon or letting in light.
A method of escape, especially an ambiguity or exception in a rule or law that can be exploited in order to avoid its effect.
To prepare a building for defense by preparing slits or holes through which to fire on attackers
To exploit (a law, etc.) by means of loopholes.
The method used to begin play in ice hockey.
To bring up to maturity, as offspring; to educate; to instruct; to foster.
(said of people towards animals) To breed and raise.
definitions.example: The family has been rearing cattle for 200 years.

definitions.definition: To rise up on the hind legs
definitions.example: The horse was shocked, and thus reared.

definitions.definition: (usually with "up") To get angry.
To rise high above, tower above.
To raise physically or metaphorically; to lift up; to cause to rise, to elevate.
definitions.example: The monster slowly reared its head.

definitions.definition: To construct by building; to set up
definitions.example: to rear defenses or houses

definitions.definition: To raise spiritually; to lift up; to elevate morally.
To lift and take up.
To rouse; to strip up.
To conclude or resolve (something):
To place or arrange in(to) a desired (especially: calm) state, or make final disposition of (something).
definitions.example: to settle her estate

definitions.definition: To become calm, quiet, or orderly; to stop being agitated.
definitions.example: the weather settled;  wait until the crowd settles before speaking

definitions.definition: To establish or become established in a steady position:
To fix one's residence in a place; to establish a dwelling place, home, or colony. (Compare settle down.)
definitions.example: the Saxons who settled in Britain

definitions.definition: To move (people) to (a land or territory), so as to colonize it; to cause (people) to take residence in (a place).
To sink, or cause (something, or impurities within it) to sink down, especially so as to become clear or compact.
To make a jointure for a spouse.
(of an animal) To make or become pregnant.
A natural attraction or feeling of kinship to a person or thing.
A family relationship through marriage of a relative (e.g. sister-in-law), as opposed to consanguinity (e.g. sister).
A kinsman or kinswoman of a such relationship; one who is affinal.
The fact of and manner in which something is related to another.
Any romantic relationship.
Any passionate love for something.
Resemblances between biological populations; resemblances that suggest that they are of a common origin, type or stock.
Structural resemblances between minerals; resemblances that suggest that they are of a common origin or type.
An attractive force between atoms, or groups of atoms, that contributes towards their forming bonds
The attraction between an antibody and an antigen
Tendency to keep a task running on the same processor in a symmetric multiprocessing operating system to reduce the frequency of cache misses
An automorphism of affine space.
A fleet of warships, especially with reference to the Spanish Armada.
Any large army or fleet of military vessels.
A large flock of anything.
definitions.example: An armada of insects attacked us every day at sunset.

definitions.definition: An animal of the subkingdom Articulata.
Clear; effective.
Speaking in a clear and effective manner.
definitions.example: She’s a bright, articulate young woman.

definitions.definition: Consisting of segments united by joints.
definitions.example: The robot arm was articulate in two directions.

definitions.definition: Distinctly marked off.
definitions.example: an articulate period in history

definitions.definition: Expressed in articles or in separate items or particulars.
(of sound) Related to human speech, as distinct from the vocalisation of animals.
A soldier, normally a member of a marine corps, trained to serve on board or from a ship
definitions.example: He was a marine in World War II.

definitions.definition: Capitalised in the plural A marine corps.
definitions.example: He fought with the Marines in World War II.

definitions.definition: A painting representing some marine subject.
Belonging to or characteristic of the sea; existing or found in the sea; formed or produced by the sea.
Relating to or connected with the sea (in operation, scope, etc.), especially as pertains to shipping, a navy, or naval forces.
Used or adapted for use at sea.
Inhabiting the high seas; oceanic; pelagic. (distinguished from maritime or littoral)
Belonging to or situated at the seaside; maritime.
The inevitable progression into the future with the passing of present and past events.
definitions.example: Time stops for nobody.   the ebb and flow of time

definitions.definition: A duration of time.
An instant of time.
The measurement under some system of region of day or moment.
definitions.example: Let's synchronize our watches so we're not on different time.

definitions.definition: Ratio of comparison.
definitions.example: your car runs three times faster than mine;  that is four times as heavy as this

definitions.definition: (grammar) Tense.
definitions.example: the time of a verb

definitions.definition: The measured duration of sounds; measure; tempo; rate of movement; rhythmical division.
definitions.example: common or triple time;   the musician keeps good time.

definitions.definition: To measure or record the time, duration, or rate of.
definitions.example: I used a stopwatch to time myself running around the block.

definitions.definition: To choose when something begins or how long it lasts.
definitions.example: The President timed his speech badly, coinciding with the Super Bowl.

definitions.definition: To keep or beat time; to proceed or move in time.
To pass time; to delay.
To regulate as to time; to accompany, or agree with, in time of movement.
To measure, as in music or harmony.
Reminder by the umpire for the players to continue playing after their pause.
The umpire's call in prizefights, etc.
A call by a bartender to warn patrons that the establishment is closing and no more drinks will be served.
The loud rumbling, cracking, or crashing sound caused by expansion of rapidly heated air around a lightning bolt.
definitions.example: Thunder is preceded by lightning.

definitions.definition: A deep, rumbling noise resembling thunder.
definitions.example: Off in the distance, he heard the thunder of hoofbeats, signalling a stampede.

definitions.definition: An alarming or startling threat or denunciation.
The discharge of electricity; a thunderbolt.
The spotlight.
definitions.example: Shortly after I announced my pregnancy, he stole my thunder with his news of landing his dream job.

definitions.definition: To produce thunder; to sound, rattle, or roar, as a discharge of atmospheric electricity; often used impersonally.
definitions.example: It thundered continuously.

definitions.definition: To make a noise like thunder.
definitions.example: The train thundered along the tracks.

definitions.definition: To talk with a loud, threatening voice.
To say (something) with a loud, threatening voice.
definitions.example: "Get back to work at once!", he thundered.

definitions.definition: To produce something with incredible power
Any of various birds of prey of the order Strigiformes that are primarily nocturnal and have forward-looking, binocular vision, limited eye movement, and good hearing.
(by extension) A person seen as having owl-like characteristics, especially appearing wise or serious, or being nocturnally active.
The owl pigeon.
A politician with moderate views that are neither hawkish nor dovish.
Any of various nymphalid butterflies having large eyespots on the wings.
Relatively large in scale, size, extent, number (i.e. having many parts or members) or duration (i.e. relatively long); very big.
definitions.example: A great storm is approaching our shores.

definitions.definition: Of larger size or more importance than others of its kind.
definitions.example: the great auk

definitions.definition: (qualifying nouns of family relationship) Involving more generations than the qualified word implies — as many extra generations as repetitions of the word great (from 1510s). [see Derived terms]
definitions.example: great-grandfather, great-great-grandfather, great-great-great-grandfather

definitions.definition: (followed by 'with') Pregnant; large with young; full of.
definitions.example: great with child

definitions.definition: (except with 'friend' and similar words such as 'mate','buddy') Intimate; familiar.
Extreme or more than usual.
definitions.example: great worry

definitions.definition: Of significant importance or consequence; important.
definitions.example: a great decision

definitions.definition: (applied to actions, thoughts and feelings) Arising from or possessing idealism; admirable; superior; commanding; heroic; illustrious; eminent.
definitions.example: a great deed

definitions.definition: Impressive or striking.
definitions.example: a great show of wealth

definitions.definition: Much in use; favoured.
definitions.example: Poetry was a great convention of the Romantic era.

definitions.definition: (applied to persons) Endowed with extraordinary powers; of exceptional talents or achievements; uncommonly gifted; able to accomplish vast results; remarkable; strong; powerful; mighty; noble.
definitions.example: a great hero, scholar, genius, philosopher, writer etc.

definitions.definition: Title referring to an important leader.
definitions.example: Alexander the Great

definitions.definition: Doing or exemplifying (a characteristic or pursuit) on a large scale; active or enthusiastic.
definitions.example: He's not a great one for reading.

definitions.definition: (often followed by 'at') Skilful or adroit.
definitions.example: You are great at singing.

definitions.definition: Very good; excellent; wonderful; fantastic (from 1848).
definitions.example: Dinner was great.

definitions.definition: Intensifying a word or expression, used in mild oaths.
definitions.example: Great Scott!
